ground_truth.title: The Year in Mathematics
ground_truth.author: Jordana Cepelewicz
ground_truth.main_content: Mathematics is, at its core, an art. Like painters, musicians or writers, mathematicians create and explore new worlds. They test, and then push past, the limits of their imagination. This artistic pursuit of beauty, truth and meaning is what every Quanta math story is about, to some extent. One of the standout stories of the year involved Hannah Cairo, a 17-year-old mathematician who solved an important problem in harmonic analysis. Cairo grew up in the Bahamas, where she was homeschooled, learning math by watching Khan Academy videos. After several months of persistent work, she constructed a counterexample to a 40-year-old conjecture that more seasoned mathematicians had missed. The year 2025 marked a historic period in mathematics, with researchers solving a major case of Hilbert's ambitious sixth problem, proving a sweeping new theorem about hyperbolic surfaces, and settling the longstanding three-dimensional Kakeya conjecture. Another remarkable development involved the 'ten martini problem,' exploring how energy levels of electrons can form a fractal pattern called the Cantor set. The legacy of Maryam Mirzakhani, the first woman to win a Fields Medal who died at age 40, continued to influence mathematics as other mathematicians picked up where she left off in understanding hyperbolic surfaces.

Cairo grew up in the Bahamas, where she was homeschooled, learning math by watching Khan Academy videos.
After several months of persistent work, she constructed a counterexample to the conjecture that more seasoned mathematicians had missed.
The year 2025 marked a historic year in mathematics. Researchers solved a major case of Hilbert's ambitious sixth problem.
The legacy of Maryam Mirzakhani continued to influence mathematics as other mathematicians picked up where she left off.
